ApiErrorDetail.description:
The error object inside the `{ "error": {...} }` envelope. May carry
additional context keys beyond the three fixed ones.

code.description: Stable machine-readable code for this specific error.
code.title: Code

ApiErrorDetail.title: ApiErrorDetail

ApiErrorResponse.description:
Envelope returned for every 4xx/5xx from /v1, except 422 request
validation errors which use FastAPI's standard `{"detail": [...]}` shape.
ApiErrorResponse.title: ApiErrorResponse

ExportCreateRequest.description:
Body for POST /v1/exports (usage-billed by result size).

One export = one dataset in one sport/division scope, written as a single
gzipped flat file. Use the optional filters to keep large datasets under
the row cap.

format.description: 'csv' or 'jsonl'; the file is always gzipped.
format.title: Format

ExportCreateRequest.title: ExportCreateRequest

ExportJobStatus.description:
Status of an export job. `download_url` appears once the job succeeds and
is minted fresh (short-lived) on every status read; re-poll for a new one.
The stored file itself expires at `expires_at`.

billed_usd.description: Amount billed for this export; present once billing lands.
billed_usd.title: Billed Usd

download_expires_at.description: When `download_url` stops working; poll again for a fresh one.
download_expires_at.title: Download Expires At

download_url.description: Short-lived signed download URL; present while status is 'succeeded'.
download_url.title: Download Url

expires_at.description: When the stored file is deleted (status becomes 'expired').
expires_at.title: Expires At

params.description: Echo of the requested scope and filters.
params.title: Params

ExportJobStatus.title: ExportJobStatus

ExportListPage.description: One page of export jobs (newest first).

has_more.description: True when another page is available.
has_more.title: Has More

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; null when there are no more results.
next_cursor.title: Next Cursor

ExportListPage.title: ExportListPage

GameFixture.description:
One game as listed by GET /v1/games — a side-anchored fixture (home/away,
never perspective-relative). A game between teams in different divisions is
visible in either division's scope.

winner_team_id.description: Null for ties (or an untracked winner).
winner_team_id.title: Winner Team Id

GameFixture.title: GameFixture

has_more.description: True when another page is available.
has_more.title: Has More

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; null when there are no more results.
next_cursor.title: Next Cursor

GameSummary.description:
A game with everything held for it, passed through under `detail`:
the fixture (`detail.game`), team/player box scores, play-by-play, and
(where covered) drives and recaps. Per-sport shapes vary.

detail.description: Complete game payload: `game` (the fixture), `team_stats`, `player_stats`, `play_by_play`, and per-sport extras (drives, recaps). Shape varies by sport.
detail.title: Detail

GameSummary.title: GameSummary

PlayerDetail.description:
A single player's profile with season stats, accolades, and (when the
player is linked to a person) their cross-program career.

career.description: Cross-program career topology (stints across schools), when linked.
career.title: Career

person_id.description: Cross-program person id; use with GET /v1/persons/{person_id}.

season_stats.description: Per-season stat rows; shape varies by sport.

PlayerSearchRequest.description:
Public body for POST /v1/players/search.

Scope (`sport`/`gender`/`division`) is required and drives RLS. The
remaining fields are a curated, sport-agnostic subset of the internal
manual-search filters; richer catalog-driven filters come in a later rev.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a prior response.
cursor.title: Cursor

division.description: Division scope; may be a comma-joined NCAA list, e.g. 'D1,D2'.
division.title: Division

PlayerSearchRequest.title: PlayerSearchRequest

PlayerSeasonsResponse.description:
Season-history view for one player. All three keys are always present;
`track_performances` is populated for track & field athletes only.

track_performances.description: Track & field performance list; null for other sports.

PlayerSummary.description:
A player row as returned by search — identity plus a sport-shaped stat
bag. Contact fields (email/phone/social) are intentionally omitted.

season.description: Season the stat bag belongs to, e.g. '2025-26'.

stats.description: Sport-shaped stat fields; keys vary by sport (see GET /v1/filters/catalog).

One live transfer-portal entry. `contacts` is present only for callers
whose owning account is PRO/MAX for the sport — the sole deliberate
exception to the no-contact-info rule, matching the in-app portal paywall.

initiated_date.description: Date the athlete entered the portal.

last_seen_at.description: When this entry was last confirmed present.

PublicQueryRequest.description:
Body for POST /v1/query (natural-language query — usage-billed).

The model is fixed server-side in v1 so billing stays predictable.

examples.prompt: Who led the NESCAC in assists this season?

Status of a natural-language query run. `answer` and `usage` appear once
the run is terminal (usage lands seconds after completion, when billing
finalizes).

examples.answer: The top three D1 scorers this season are ...

answer.description: Final answer text; present once the run is done.
answer.title: Answer

usage.description: Token counts, model, and billed amount; present once billing finalizes.

ApiKeyAuth.description: API key created at https://magisterial.ai/console/api-keys, sent as `Authorization: Bearer mag_live_...`.

info.description:
REST API for college sports data across NCAA D1/D2/D3, NAIA, and NJCAA:
players, teams, rosters, cross-program careers, games, the live transfer
portal, and an agent-backed natural-language query endpoint.

## Authentication

Every endpoint requires an API key sent as a bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer mag_live_...
```

Create and manage keys in the developer console at
[magisterial.ai/console/api-keys](https://magisterial.ai/console/api-keys).
Keys are prefixed `mag_live_` (production) or `mag_test_`.

## Scoping

Data access is scoped by `sport`, `division`, and (for dual-gender sports)
`gender`. Valid values come from `GET /v1/sports` and `GET /v1/divisions`;
some endpoints accept comma-joined NCAA divisions, e.g. `D1,D2`.

## Pagination

List endpoints paginate with an opaque `cursor`. Pass the `next_cursor` from
one response as the `cursor` of the next request; `has_more` signals whether
another page exists.

## Errors

Every 4xx/5xx returns a JSON envelope:

```json
{ "error": { "type": "invalid_request", "code": "invalid_scope", "message": "..." } }
```

The one exception is request validation (422), which uses FastAPI's standard
`{"detail": [...]}` shape. Rate-limited responses (429) include
`X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset`, and
`Retry-After` headers.

## Billing

Endpoints marked *usage-billed* (`/v1/portal`, `/v1/query`, `/v1/alerts`,
`/v1/exports`) require API billing to be enabled on the account and meter
usage against a monthly budget; the free endpoints are included with any API
key. A 402 response means billing is not enabled or the budget is exhausted.

## Bulk exports

For full-dataset pulls, use the bulk lane instead of walking paginated
endpoints: `POST /v1/exports` starts an async job that writes one dataset
(players, teams, games, or coaches) in one sport/division scope to a gzipped
CSV or JSONL file, and `GET /v1/exports/{export_id}` returns a short-lived
signed download URL once it succeeds. Billed on success by result size
(per started 10,000-row block); failed exports are never billed.

## Webhooks

Instead of polling, register webhook endpoints in the
[console](https://magisterial.ai/console/webhooks) to receive signed events:
`alert.triggered` when a watch alert finds new portal matches, and
`query.completed` when an async query run finishes. Payloads carry a
`Magisterial-Signature` header (`t=<ts>,v1=<hmac_sha256_hex>` over
`{t}.{raw_body}`, keyed with the endpoint's `whsec_` secret); failed
deliveries retry with backoff for about two days. See the webhooks guide at
[magisterial.ai/docs](https://magisterial.ai/docs#webhooks-guide).

get.description:
All alerts created through the API for this account, newest first.
Alerts created in the app are not visible here.

401.description: Missing, malformed, or revoked API key.

402.description: API billing not enabled for this account, or the monthly budget is exhausted.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. See the X-RateLimit-* and Retry-After headers.

post.description:
Watch the transfer portal for entries matching a filter set. Billed per
active alert per calendar month, charged on creation. Matches push to
webhook endpoints registered in the console (event `alert.triggered`);
GET /v1/alerts/{alert_id}/matches remains available for polling.

402.description: API billing not enabled for this account, or the monthly budget is exhausted.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. See the X-RateLimit-* and Retry-After headers.

refunded, and recreating within the same month does not double-charge.

download URL (most exports finish in well under a minute). Billed on
